Test ID: B107
Severity: LOW
Confidence: MEDIUM
CWE: CWE-259
File: .\.venv\Lib\site-packages\autobahn\wamp\mnemonic.py
Line number: 160
More info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
159	
160	def mnemonic_to_private_key(
161	    mnemonic, str_derivation_path=LEDGER_ETH_DERIVATION_PATH, passphrase=""
162	):
163	    """Performs all convertions to get a private key from a mnemonic sentence.
164	
165	    This includes:
166	
167	    - BIP39 mnemonic to seed
168	    - BIP32 seed to master key
169	    - BIP32 child derivation of a path provided
170	
171	    :param mnemonic: seed wordlist, usually with 24 words, that is used for ledger wallet backup
172	    :param str_derivation_path: string that directs BIP32 key derivation, defaults to path used by ledger ETH wallet
173	    :param passphrase: optional passphrase for BIP39 seed generation
174	
175	    """
176	
177	    derivation_path = parse_derivation_path(str_derivation_path)
178	
179	    bip39seed = mnemonic_to_bip39seed(mnemonic, passphrase)
180	
181	    master_private_key, master_chain_code = bip39seed_to_bip32masternode(bip39seed)
182	
183	    private_key, chain_code = master_private_key, master_chain_code
184	
185	    for i in derivation_path:
186	        private_key, chain_code = derive_bip32childkey(private_key, chain_code, i)
187	
188	    return private_key

109	
110	
111	def register_feature_id(feature_id):
112	    """Adds metric value to the current context object's ``features`` set.
113	
114	    :type feature_id: str
115	    :param feature_id: The name of the feature to register. Value must be a key
116	        in the ``_USERAGENT_FEATURE_MAPPINGS`` dict.
117	    """
118	    ctx = get_context()
119	    if ctx is None:
120	        # Never register features outside the scope of a
121	        # ``botocore.context.start_as_current_context`` context manager.
122	        # Otherwise, the context variable won't be reset and features will
123	        # bleed into all subsequent requests. Return instead of raising an
124	        # exception since this function could be invoked in a public interface.
125	        return
126	    if val := _USERAGENT_FEATURE_MAPPINGS.get(feature_id):
127	        ctx.features.add(val)
128	
129	
130	def register_feature_ids(feature_ids):
131	    """Adds multiple feature IDs to the current context object's ``features`` set.
132	
133	    :type feature_ids: iterable of str
134	    :param feature_ids: An iterable of feature ID strings to register. Each
135	        value must be a key in the ``_USERAGENT_FEATURE_MAPPINGS`` dict.

106	    'FLEXIBLE_CHECKSUMS_REQ_XXHASH64': 'AH',
107	    'FLEXIBLE_CHECKSUMS_REQ_XXHASH128': 'AI',
108	}
109	
110	
111	def register_feature_id(feature_id):
112	    """Adds metric value to the current context object's ``features`` set.
113	
114	    :type feature_id: str
115	    :param feature_id: The name of the feature to register. Value must be a key
116	        in the ``_USERAGENT_FEATURE_MAPPINGS`` dict.
117	    """
118	    ctx = get_context()
119	    if ctx is None:
120	        # Never register features outside the scope of a
121	        # ``botocore.context.start_as_current_context`` context manager.
122	        # Otherwise, the context variable won't be reset and features will
123	        # bleed into all subsequent requests. Return instead of raising an
124	        # exception since this function could be invoked in a public interface.
125	        return
126	    if val := _USERAGENT_FEATURE_MAPPINGS.get(feature_id):
127	        ctx.features.add(val)
128	
129	
130	def register_feature_ids(feature_ids):
131	    """Adds multiple feature IDs to the current context object's ``features`` set.
132	
133	    :type feature_ids: iterable of str
134	    :param feature_ids: An iterable of feature ID strings to register. Each
135	        value must be a key in the ``_USERAGENT_FEATURE_MAPPINGS`` dict.
136	    """
137	    for feature_id in feature_ids:
138	        register_feature_id(feature_id)
139

Test ID: B105

File: .\.venv\Lib\site-packages\drf_spectacular\settings.py
Line number: 228
More info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b105_hardcoded_password_string.html
227	    'OAUTH2_AUTHORIZATION_URL': None,
228	    'OAUTH2_TOKEN_URL': None,
229	    'OAUTH2_REFRESH_URL': None,
230	    'OAUTH2_SCOPES': None,
231	}
232	
233	IMPORT_STRINGS = [
234	    'DEFAULT_GENERATOR_CLASS',
235	    'SERVE_AUTHENTICATION',
236	    'SERVE_PERMISSIONS',
237	    'POSTPROCESSING_HOOKS',
238	    'PREPROCESSING_HOOKS',
239	    'GET_LIB_DOC_EXCLUDES',
240	    'GET_MOCK_REQUEST',
241	    'SORT_OPERATIONS',
242	    'SORT_OPERATION_PARAMETERS',
243	    'AUTHENTICATION_WHITELIST',
244	    'RENDERER_WHITELIST',
245	    'PARSER_WHITELIST',
246	    'WEBHOOKS',
247	]
248	
249	
250	class SpectacularSettings(APISettings):
251	    _original_settings: Dict[str, Any] = {}
252	
253	    def apply_patches(self, patches):
254	        for attr, val in patches.items():
255	            if attr.startswith('SERVE_') or attr == 'DEFAULT_GENERATOR_CLASS':
256	                raise AttributeError(
257	                    f'{attr} not allowed in custom_settings. use dedicated parameter instead.'
258	                )
259	            if attr in self.import_strings:
260	                val = perform_import(val, attr)
261	            # load and store original value, then override __dict__ entry
262	            self._original_settings[attr] = getattr(self, attr)
263	            setattr(self, attr, val)
264	
265	    def clear_patches(self):
266	        for attr, orig_val in self._original_settings.items():
267	            setattr(self, attr, orig_val)
268	        self._original_settings = {}
269	
270	
271	spectacular_settings = SpectacularSettings(
272	    user_settings=getattr(settings, 'SPECTACULAR_SETTINGS', {}),  # type: ignore
273	    defaults=SPECTACULAR_DEFAULTS,  # type: ignore
274	    import_strings=IMPORT_STRINGS,
275	)
276	
277	
278	@contextmanager
279	def patched_settings(patches):
280	    """ temporarily patch the global spectacular settings (or do nothing) """
281	    if not patches:
282	        yield
283	    else:
284	        try:
285	            spectacular_settings.apply_patches(patches)
286	            yield
287	        finally:
288	            spectacular_settings.clear_patches()

hardcoded_password_default: Possible hardcoded password: '/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token'
Test ID: B107
Severity: LOW
Confidence: MEDIUM
CWE: CWE-259
File: .\.venv\Lib\site-packages\openai\auth\_workload.py
Line number: 43
More info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
42	
43	def k8s_service_account_token_provider(
44	    token_file_path: str | Path = "/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token",
45	) -> SubjectTokenProvider:
46	    """
47	    Get a subject token provider for Kubernetes clusters with Workload Identity configured.
48	
49	    Cloud providers typically mount the subject token as a file in the container.
50	
51	    Args:
52	        token_file_path: path to the mounted service account token file. Defaults to `/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token`.
53	    """
54	
55	    def get_token() -> str:
56	        try:
57	            with open(token_file_path, "r") as f:
58	                token = f.read().strip()
59	                if not token:
60	                    raise SubjectTokenProviderError(f"The token file at {token_file_path} is empty.")
61	                return token
62	        except Exception as e:
63	            raise SubjectTokenProviderError(f"Failed to read the token file at {token_file_path}: {e}") from e
64	
65	    return {"token_type": "jwt", "get_token": get_token}
66

Test ID: B107
Severity: LOW
Confidence: MEDIUM
CWE: CWE-259
File: .\.venv\Lib\site-packages\openpyxl\utils\protection.py
Line number: 4
More info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
3	
4	def hash_password(plaintext_password=''):
5	    """
6	    Create a password hash from a given string for protecting a worksheet
7	    only. This will not work for encrypting a workbook.
8	
9	    This method is based on the algorithm provided by
10	    Daniel Rentz of OpenOffice and the PEAR package
11	    Spreadsheet_Excel_Writer by Xavier Noguer <xnoguer@rezebra.com>.
12	    See also http://blogs.msdn.com/b/ericwhite/archive/2008/02/23/the-legacy-hashing-algorithm-in-open-xml.aspx
13	    """
14	    password = 0x0000
15	    for idx, char in enumerate(plaintext_password, 1):
16	        value = ord(char) << idx
17	        rotated_bits = value >> 15
18	        value &= 0x7fff
19	        password ^= (value | rotated_bits)
20	    password ^= len(plaintext_password)
21	    password ^= 0xCE4B
22	    return str(hex(password)).upper()[2:]

135	# List of settings that may be in string import notation.
136	IMPORT_STRINGS = [
137	    'DEFAULT_RENDERER_CLASSES',
138	    'DEFAULT_PARSER_CLASSES',
139	    'DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES',
140	    'DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES',
141	    'DEFAULT_THROTTLE_CLASSES',

144	    'DEFAULT_VERSIONING_CLASS',

146	    'DEFAULT_FILTER_BACKENDS',
147	    'DEFAULT_SCHEMA_CLASS',
148	    'EXCEPTION_HANDLER',
149	    'TEST_REQUEST_RENDERER_CLASSES',
150	    'UNAUTHENTICATED_USER',
151	    'UNAUTHENTICATED_TOKEN',
152	    'VIEW_NAME_FUNCTION',
153	    'VIEW_DESCRIPTION_FUNCTION'
154	]
155	
156	
157	# List of settings that have been removed
158	REMOVED_SETTINGS = [
159	    'PAGINATE_BY', 'PAGINATE_BY_PARAM', 'MAX_PAGINATE_BY',
160	]
161	
162	
163	def perform_import(val, setting_name):
164	    """
165	    If the given setting is a string import notation,
166	    then perform the necessary import or imports.
167	    """
168	    if val is None:
169	        return None
170	    elif isinstance(val, str):
171	        return import_from_string(val, setting_name)
172	    elif isinstance(val, (list, tuple)):
173	        return [import_from_string(item, setting_name) for item in val]
174	    return val
175	
176	
177	def import_from_string(val, setting_name):
178	    """
179	    Attempt to import a class from a string representation.
180	    """
181	    try:
182	        return import_string(val)
183	    except ImportError as e:
184	        msg = "Could not import '%s' for API setting '%s'. %s: %s." % (val, setting_name, e.__class__.__name__, e)

675	
676	    def test_fromFile(self):
677	        """
678	        Test that fromFile works correctly.
679	        """
680	        self.assertEqual(
681	            keys.Key.fromFile(self.keyFile), keys.Key.fromString(keydata.privateRSA_lsh)
682	        )
683	        self.assertRaises(keys.BadKeyError, keys.Key.fromFile, self.keyFile, "bad_type")
684	        self.assertRaises(
685	            keys.BadKeyError, keys.Key.fromFile, self.keyFile, passphrase="unencrypted"
686	        )
687	
688	    def test_init(self):
689	        """
690	        Test that the PublicKey object is initialized correctly.
691	        """
692	        obj = keys.Key._fromRSAComponents(n=5, e=3)._keyObject
693	        key = keys.Key(obj)
694	        self.assertEqual(key._keyObject, obj)
695	
696	    def test_equal(self):
697	        """
698	        Test that Key objects are compared correctly.
699	        """
700	        rsa1 = keys.Key(self.rsaObj)
701	        rsa2 = keys.Key(self.rsaObj)
702	        rsa3 = keys.Key(keys.Key._fromRSAComponents(n=5, e=3)._keyObject)
703	        dsa = keys.Key(self.dsaObj)
704	        self.assertTrue(rsa1 == rsa2)
705	        self.assertFalse(rsa1 == rsa3)
706	        self.assertFalse(rsa1 == dsa)
707	        self.assertFalse(rsa1 == object)
708	        self.assertFalse(rsa1 == None)

69	
70	    def addUser(
71	        self,
72	        username: str,
73	        password: str = "password",
74	        uid: int | None = None,
75	        gid: int | None = None,
76	        gecos: str = "",
77	        home: str = "",
78	        shell: str = "/bin/sh",
79	    ) -> None:
80	        """
81	        Add a new user record to this database.
82	
83	        @param username: The value for the C{pw_name} field of the user
84	            record to add.
85	
86	        @param password: The value for the C{pw_passwd} field of the user
87	            record to add.
88	
89	        @param uid: The value for the C{pw_uid} field of the user record to
90	            add.
91	
92	        @param gid: The value for the C{pw_gid} field of the user record to
93	            add.
94	
95	        @param gecos: The value for the C{pw_gecos} field of the user record
96	            to add.
97	
98	        @param home: The value for the C{pw_dir} field of the user record to
99	            add.
100	
101	        @param shell: The value for the C{pw_shell} field of the user record to
102	            add.
103	        """
104	        if uid is None:
105	            uid = self._lastUID
106	            self._lastUID += 1
107	        if gid is None:
108	            gid = self._lastGID
109	            self._lastGID += 1
110	        newUser = _UserRecord(username, password, uid, gid, gecos, home, shell)
111	        self._users.append(newUser)

412	        self.addBootstrap(xmlstream.STREAM_CONNECTED_EVENT, self.onConnectionMade)
413	        self.addBootstrap(xmlstream.STREAM_AUTHD_EVENT, self.onAuthenticated)
414	
415	        self.serial = 0

43	
44	exec(
45	    r'''
46	import sys, re
47	
48	MIN_WINDOW=4096
49	# File lookup window
50	
51	class SyntaxError(Exception):
52	    """When we run into an unexpected token, this is the exception to use"""
53	    def __init__(self, pos=None, msg="Bad Token", context=None):
54	        Exception.__init__(self)
55	        self.pos = pos
56	        self.msg = msg
57	        self.context = context
58	
59	    def __str__(self):
60	        if not self.pos: return 'SyntaxError'
61	        else: return 'SyntaxError@%s(%s)' % (repr(self.pos), self.msg)
62	
63	class NoMoreTokens(Exception):
64	    """Another exception object, for when we run out of tokens"""
65	    pass
66	
67	class Token:
68	    """Yapps token.
69	
70	    This is a container for a scanned token.
71	    """
72	
73	    def __init__(self, type,value, pos=None):
74	        """Initialize a token."""
75	        self.type = type
76	        self.value = value
77	        self.pos = pos
78	
79	    def __repr__(self):
80	        output = '<%s: %s' % (self.type, repr(self.value))
81	        if self.pos:
82	            output += " @ "
83	            if self.pos[0]:
84	                output += "%s:" % self.pos[0]
85	            if self.pos[1]:
86	                output += "%d" % self.pos[1]
87	            if self.pos[2] is not None:
88	                output += ".%d" % self.pos[2]
89	        output += ">"
90	        return output
91	
92	in_name=0
93	class Scanner:
94	    """Yapps scanner.
95	
96	    The Yapps scanner can work in context sensitive or context
97	    insensitive modes.  The token(i) method is used to retrieve the
98	    i-th token.  It takes a restrict set that limits the set of tokens
99	    it is allowed to return.  In context sensitive mode, this restrict
100	    set guides the scanner.  In context insensitive mode, there is no
101	    restriction (the set is always the full set of tokens).
102	
103	    """
104	
105	    def __init__(self, patterns, ignore, input="",
106	            file=None,filename=None,stacked=False):
107	        """Initialize the scanner.
108	
109	        Parameters:
110	          patterns : [(terminal, uncompiled regex), ...] or None
111	          ignore : {terminal:None, ...}
112	          input : string
113	
114	        If patterns is None, we assume that the subclass has
115	        defined self.patterns : [(terminal, compiled regex), ...].
116	        Note that the patterns parameter expects uncompiled regexes,
117	        whereas the self.patterns field expects compiled regexes.
118	
119	        The 'ignore' value is either None or a callable, which is called
120	        with the scanner and the to-be-ignored match object; this can
121	        be used for include file or comment handling.
122	        """
123	
124	        if not filename:
125	            global in_name
126	            filename="<f.%d>" % in_name
127	            in_name += 1
128	
129	        self.input = input
130	        self.ignore = ignore
131	        self.file = file
132	        self.filename = filename
133	        self.pos = 0
134	        self.del_pos = 0 # skipped
135	        self.line = 1
136	        self.del_line = 0 # skipped
137	        self.col = 0
138	        self.tokens = []
139	        self.stack = None
140	        self.stacked = stacked
141	
142	        self.last_read_token = None
143	        self.last_token = None
144	        self.last_types = None
145	
146	        if patterns is not None:
147	            # Compile the regex strings into regex objects
148	            self.patterns = []
149	            for terminal, regex in patterns:
150	                self.patterns.append( (terminal, re.compile(regex)) )
151	
152	    def stack_input(self, input="", file=None, filename=None):
153	        """Temporarily parse from a second file."""
154	
155	        # Already reading from somewhere else: Go on top of that, please.
156	        if self.stack:
157	            # autogenerate a recursion-level-identifying filename
158	            if not filename:
159	                filename = 1
160	            else:
161	                try:
162	                    filename += 1
163	                except TypeError:
164	                    pass
165	                # now pass off to the include file
166	            self.stack.stack_input(input,file,filename)
167	        else:
168	
169	            try:
170	                filename += 0
171	            except TypeError:
172	                pass
173	            else:
174	                filename = "<str_%d>" % filename
175	
176	#			self.stack = object.__new__(self.__class__)
177	#			Scanner.__init__(self.stack,self.patterns,self.ignore,input,file,filename, stacked=True)
178	
179	            # Note that the pattern+ignore are added by the generated
180	            # scanner code
181	            self.stack = self.__class__(input,file,filename, stacked=True)
182	
183	    def get_pos(self):
184	        """Return a file/line/char tuple."""
185	        if self.stack: return self.stack.get_pos()
186	
187	        return (self.filename, self.line+self.del_line, self.col)
188	
189	#	def __repr__(self):
190	#		"""Print the last few tokens that have been scanned in"""
191	#		output = ''
192	#		for t in self.tokens:
193	#			output += '%s\n' % (repr(t),)
194	#		return output
195	
196	    def print_line_with_pointer(self, pos, length=0, out=sys.stderr):
197	        """Print the line of 'text' that includes position 'p',
198	        along with a second line with a single caret (^) at position p"""
199	
200	        file,line,p = pos
201	        if file != self.filename:
202	            if self.stack: return self.stack.print_line_with_pointer(pos,length=length,out=out)
203	            print >>out, "(%s: not in input buffer)" % file
204	            return
205	
206	        text = self.input
207	        p += length-1 # starts at pos 1
208	
209	        origline=line
210	        line -= self.del_line
211	        spos=0
212	        if line > 0:
213	            while 1:
214	                line = line - 1
215	                try:
216	                    cr = text.index("\n",spos)
217	                except ValueError:
218	                    if line:
219	                        text = ""
220	                    break
221	                if line == 0:
222	                    text = text[spos:cr]
223	                    break
224	                spos = cr+1
225	        else:
226	            print >>out, "(%s:%d not in input buffer)" % (file,origline)
227	            return
228	
229	        # Now try printing part of the line
230	        text = text[max(p-80, 0):p+80]
231	        p = p - max(p-80, 0)
232	
233	        # Strip to the left
234	        i = text[:p].rfind('\n')
235	        j = text[:p].rfind('\r')
236	        if i < 0 or (0 <= j < i): i = j
237	        if 0 <= i < p:
238	            p = p - i - 1
239	            text = text[i+1:]
240	
241	        # Strip to the right
242	        i = text.find('\n', p)
243	        j = text.find('\r', p)
244	        if i < 0 or (0 <= j < i): i = j
245	        if i >= 0:
246	            text = text[:i]
247	
248	        # Now shorten the text
249	        while len(text) > 70 and p > 60:
250	            # Cut off 10 chars
251	            text = "..." + text[10:]
252	            p = p - 7
253	
254	        # Now print the string, along with an indicator
255	        print >>out, '> ',text
256	        print >>out, '> ',' '*p + '^'
257	
258	    def grab_input(self):
259	        """Get more input if possible."""
260	        if not self.file: return
261	        if len(self.input) - self.pos >= MIN_WINDOW: return
262	
263	        data = self.file.read(MIN_WINDOW)
264	        if data is None or data == "":
265	            self.file = None
266	
267	        # Drop bytes from the start, if necessary.
268	        if self.pos > 2*MIN_WINDOW:
269	            self.del_pos += MIN_WINDOW
270	            self.del_line += self.input[:MIN_WINDOW].count("\n")
271	            self.pos -= MIN_WINDOW
272	            self.input = self.input[MIN_WINDOW:] + data
273	        else:
274	            self.input = self.input + data
275	
276	    def getchar(self):
277	        """Return the next character."""
278	        self.grab_input()
279	
280	        c = self.input[self.pos]
281	        self.pos += 1
282	        return c
283	
284	    def token(self, restrict, context=None):
285	        """Scan for another token."""
286	
287	        while 1:
288	            if self.stack:
289	                try:
290	                    return self.stack.token(restrict, context)
291	                except StopIteration:
292	                    self.stack = None
293	
294	        # Keep looking for a token, ignoring any in self.ignore
295	            self.grab_input()
296	
297	            # special handling for end-of-file
298	            if self.stacked and self.pos==len(self.input):
299	                raise StopIteration
300	
301	            # Search the patterns for the longest match, with earlier
302	            # tokens in the list having preference
303	            best_match = -1
304	            best_pat = '(error)'
305	            best_m = None
306	            for p, regexp in self.patterns:
307	                # First check to see if we're ignoring this token
308	                if restrict and p not in restrict and p not in self.ignore:
309	                    continue
310	                m = regexp.match(self.input, self.pos)
311	                if m and m.end()-m.start() > best_match:
312	                    # We got a match that's better than the previous one
313	                    best_pat = p
314	                    best_match = m.end()-m.start()
315	                    best_m = m
316	
317	            # If we didn't find anything, raise an error
318	            if best_pat == '(error)' and best_match < 0:
319	                msg = 'Bad Token'
320	                if restrict:
321	                    msg = 'Trying to find one of '+', '.join(restrict)
322	                raise SyntaxError(self.get_pos(), msg, context=context)
323	
324	            ignore = best_pat in self.ignore
325	            value = self.input[self.pos:self.pos+best_match]
326	            if not ignore:
327	                tok=Token(type=best_pat, value=value, pos=self.get_pos())
328	
329	            self.pos += best_match
330	
331	            npos = value.rfind("\n")
332	            if npos > -1:
333	                self.col = best_match-npos
334	                self.line += value.count("\n")
335	            else:
336	                self.col += best_match
337	
338	            # If we found something that isn't to be ignored, return it
339	            if not ignore:
340	                if len(self.tokens) >= 10:
341	                    del self.tokens[0]
342	                self.tokens.append(tok)
343	                self.last_read_token = tok
344	                # print repr(tok)
345	                return tok
346	            else:
347	                ignore = self.ignore[best_pat]
348	                if ignore:
349	                    ignore(self, best_m)
350	
351	    def peek(self, *types, **kw):
352	        """Returns the token type for lookahead; if there are any args
353	        then the list of args is the set of token types to allow"""
354	        context = kw.get("context",None)
355	        if self.last_token is None:
356	            self.last_types = types
357	            self.last_token = self.token(types,context)
358	        elif self.last_types:
359	            for t in types:
360	                if t not in self.last_types:
361	                    raise NotImplementedError("Unimplemented: restriction set changed")
362	        return self.last_token.type
363	
364	    def scan(self, type, **kw):
365	        """Returns the matched text, and moves to the next token"""
366	        context = kw.get("context",None)
367	
368	        if self.last_token is None:
369	            tok = self.token([type],context)
370	        else:
371	            if self.last_types and type not in self.last_types:
372	                raise NotImplementedError("Unimplemented: restriction set changed")
373	
374	            tok = self.last_token
375	            self.last_token = None
376	        if tok.type != type:
377	            if not self.last_types: self.last_types=[]
378	            raise SyntaxError(tok.pos, 'Trying to find '+type+': '+ ', '.join(self.last_types)+", got "+tok.type, context=context)
379	        return tok.value
380	
381	class Parser:
382	    """Base class for Yapps-generated parsers.
383	
384	    """
385	
386	    def __init__(self, scanner):
387	        self._scanner = scanner
388	
389	    def _stack(self, input="",file=None,filename=None):
390	        """Temporarily read from someplace else"""
391	        self._scanner.stack_input(input,file,filename)
392	        self._tok = None
393	
394	    def _peek(self, *types, **kw):
395	        """Returns the token type for lookahead; if there are any args
396	        then the list of args is the set of token types to allow"""
397	        return self._scanner.peek(*types, **kw)
398	
399	    def _scan(self, type, **kw):
400	        """Returns the matched text, and moves to the next token"""
401	        return self._scanner.scan(type, **kw)
402	
403	class Context:
404	    """Class to represent the parser's call stack.
405	
406	    Every rule creates a Context that links to its parent rule.  The
407	    contexts can be used for debugging.
408	
409	    """
410	
411	    def __init__(self, parent, scanner, rule, args=()):
412	        """Create a new context.
413	
414	        Args:
415	        parent: Context object or None
416	        scanner: Scanner object
417	        rule: string (name of the rule)
418	        args: tuple listing parameters to the rule
419	
420	        """
421	        self.parent = parent
422	        self.scanner = scanner
423	        self.rule = rule
424	        self.args = args
425	        while scanner.stack: scanner = scanner.stack
426	        self.token = scanner.last_read_token
427	
428	    def __str__(self):
429	        output = ''
430	        if self.parent: output = str(self.parent) + ' > '
431	        output += self.rule
432	        return output
433	
434	def print_error(err, scanner, max_ctx=None):
435	    """Print error messages, the parser stack, and the input text -- for human-readable error messages."""
436	    # NOTE: this function assumes 80 columns :-(
437	    # Figure out the line number
438	    pos = err.pos
439	    if not pos:
440	        pos = scanner.get_pos()
441	
442	    file_name, line_number, column_number = pos
443	    print('%s:%d:%d: %s' % (file_name, line_number, column_number, err.msg), file=sys.stderr)
444	
445	    scanner.print_line_with_pointer(pos)
446	
447	    context = err.context
448	    token = None
449	    while context:
450	        print('while parsing %s%s:' % (context.rule, tuple(context.args)), file=sys.stderr)
451	        if context.token:
452	            token = context.token
453	        if token:
454	            scanner.print_line_with_pointer(token.pos, length=len(token.value))
455	        context = context.parent
456	        if max_ctx:
457	            max_ctx = max_ctx-1
458	            if not max_ctx:
459	                break
460	
461	def wrap_error_reporter(parser, rule, *args,**kw):
462	    try:
463	        return getattr(parser, rule)(*args,**kw)
464	    except SyntaxError as e:
465	        print_error(e, parser._scanner)
466	    except NoMoreTokens:
467	        print('Could not complete parsing; stopped around here:', file=sys.stderr)
468	        print(parser._scanner, file=sys.stderr)
469	
470	from twisted.words.xish.xpath import AttribValue, BooleanValue, CompareValue
471	from twisted.words.xish.xpath import Function, IndexValue, LiteralValue
472	from twisted.words.xish.xpath import _AnyLocation, _Location
473	
474	
475	# Begin -- grammar generated by Yapps
476	
477	class XPathParserScanner(Scanner):
478	    patterns = [
479	        ('","', re.compile(',')),
480	        ('"@"', re.compile('@')),
481	        ('"\\)"', re.compile('\\)')),
482	        ('"\\("', re.compile('\\(')),
483	        ('"\\]"', re.compile('\\]')),
484	        ('"\\["', re.compile('\\[')),
485	        ('"//"', re.compile('//')),
486	        ('"/"', re.compile('/')),
487	        ('\\s+', re.compile('\\s+')),
488	        ('INDEX', re.compile('[0-9]+')),
489	        ('WILDCARD', re.compile('\\*')),
490	        ('IDENTIFIER', re.compile('[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_\\-]*')),
491	        ('ATTRIBUTE', re.compile('\\@[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_\\-]*')),
492	        ('FUNCNAME', re.compile('[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*')),
493	        ('CMP_EQ', re.compile('\\=')),
494	        ('CMP_NE', re.compile('\\!\\=')),
495	        ('STR_DQ', re.compile('"([^"]|(\\"))*?"')),
496	        ('STR_SQ', re.compile("'([^']|(\\'))*?'")),
497	        ('OP_AND', re.compile('and')),
498	        ('OP_OR', re.compile('or')),
499	        ('END', re.compile('$')),
500	    ]
501	    def __init__(self, str,*args,**kw):
502	        Scanner.__init__(self,None,{'\\s+':None,},str,*args,**kw)
503	
504	class XPathParser(Parser):
505	    Context = Context
506	    def XPATH(self, _parent=None):
507	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'XPATH', [])
508	        PATH = self.PATH(_context)
509	        result = PATH; current = result
510	        while self._peek('END', '"/"', '"//"', context=_context) != 'END':
511	            PATH = self.PATH(_context)
512	            current.childLocation = PATH; current = current.childLocation
513	        END = self._scan('END', context=_context)
514	        return  result
515	
516	    def PATH(self, _parent=None):
517	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'PATH', [])
518	        _token = self._peek('"/"', '"//"', context=_context)
519	        if _token == '"/"':
520	            self._scan('"/"', context=_context)
521	            result = _Location()
522	        else: # == '"//"'
523	            self._scan('"//"', context=_context)
524	            result = _AnyLocation()
525	        _token = self._peek('IDENTIFIER', 'WILDCARD', context=_context)
526	        if _token == 'IDENTIFIER':
527	            IDENTIFIER = self._scan('IDENTIFIER', context=_context)
528	            result.elementName = IDENTIFIER
529	        else: # == 'WILDCARD'
530	            WILDCARD = self._scan('WILDCARD', context=_context)
531	            result.elementName = None
532	        while self._peek('"\\["', 'END', '"/"', '"//"', context=_context) == '"\\["':
533	            self._scan('"\\["', context=_context)
534	            PREDICATE = self.PREDICATE(_context)
535	            result.predicates.append(PREDICATE)
536	            self._scan('"\\]"', context=_context)
537	        return result
538	
539	    def PREDICATE(self, _parent=None):
540	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'PREDICATE', [])
541	        _token = self._peek('INDEX', '"\\("', '"@"', 'FUNCNAME', 'STR_DQ', 'STR_SQ', context=_context)
542	        if _token != 'INDEX':
543	            EXPR = self.EXPR(_context)
544	            return EXPR
545	        else: # == 'INDEX'
546	            INDEX = self._scan('INDEX', context=_context)
547	            return IndexValue(INDEX)
548	
549	    def EXPR(self, _parent=None):
550	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'EXPR', [])
551	        FACTOR = self.FACTOR(_context)
552	        e = FACTOR
553	        while self._peek('OP_AND', 'OP_OR', '"\\)"', '"\\]"', context=_context) in ['OP_AND', 'OP_OR']:
554	            BOOLOP = self.BOOLOP(_context)
555	            FACTOR = self.FACTOR(_context)
556	            e = BooleanValue(e, BOOLOP, FACTOR)
557	        return e
558	
559	    def BOOLOP(self, _parent=None):
560	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'BOOLOP', [])
561	        _token = self._peek('OP_AND', 'OP_OR', context=_context)
562	        if _token == 'OP_AND':
563	            OP_AND = self._scan('OP_AND', context=_context)
564	            return OP_AND
565	        else: # == 'OP_OR'
566	            OP_OR = self._scan('OP_OR', context=_context)
567	            return OP_OR
568	
569	    def FACTOR(self, _parent=None):
570	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'FACTOR', [])
571	        _token = self._peek('"\\("', '"@"', 'FUNCNAME', 'STR_DQ', 'STR_SQ', context=_context)
572	        if _token != '"\\("':
573	            TERM = self.TERM(_context)
574	            return TERM
575	        else: # == '"\\("'
576	            self._scan('"\\("', context=_context)
577	            EXPR = self.EXPR(_context)
578	            self._scan('"\\)"', context=_context)
579	            return EXPR
580	
581	    def TERM(self, _parent=None):
582	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'TERM', [])
583	        VALUE = self.VALUE(_context)
584	        t = VALUE
585	        if self._peek('CMP_EQ', 'CMP_NE', 'OP_AND', 'OP_OR', '"\\)"', '"\\]"', context=_context) in ['CMP_EQ', 'CMP_NE']:
586	            CMP = self.CMP(_context)
587	            VALUE = self.VALUE(_context)
588	            t = CompareValue(t, CMP, VALUE)
589	        return t
590	
591	    def VALUE(self, _parent=None):
592	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'VALUE', [])
593	        _token = self._peek('"@"', 'FUNCNAME', 'STR_DQ', 'STR_SQ', context=_context)
594	        if _token == '"@"':
595	            self._scan('"@"', context=_context)
596	            IDENTIFIER = self._scan('IDENTIFIER', context=_context)
597	            return AttribValue(IDENTIFIER)
598	        elif _token == 'FUNCNAME':
599	            FUNCNAME = self._scan('FUNCNAME', context=_context)
600	            f = Function(FUNCNAME); args = []
601	            self._scan('"\\("', context=_context)
602	            if self._peek('"\\)"', '"@"', 'FUNCNAME', '","', 'STR_DQ', 'STR_SQ', context=_context) not in ['"\\)"', '","']:
603	                VALUE = self.VALUE(_context)
604	                args.append(VALUE)
605	                while self._peek('","', '"\\)"', context=_context) == '","':
606	                    self._scan('","', context=_context)
607	                    VALUE = self.VALUE(_context)
608	                    args.append(VALUE)
609	            self._scan('"\\)"', context=_context)
610	            f.setParams(*args); return f
611	        else: # in ['STR_DQ', 'STR_SQ']
612	            STR = self.STR(_context)
613	            return LiteralValue(STR[1:len(STR)-1])
614	
615	    def CMP(self, _parent=None):
616	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'CMP', [])
617	        _token = self._peek('CMP_EQ', 'CMP_NE', context=_context)
618	        if _token == 'CMP_EQ':
619	            CMP_EQ = self._scan('CMP_EQ', context=_context)
620	            return CMP_EQ
621	        else: # == 'CMP_NE'
622	            CMP_NE = self._scan('CMP_NE', context=_context)
623	            return CMP_NE
624	
625	    def STR(self, _parent=None):
626	        _context = self.Context(_parent, self._scanner, 'STR', [])
627	        _token = self._peek('STR_DQ', 'STR_SQ', context=_context)
628	        if _token == 'STR_DQ':
629	            STR_DQ = self._scan('STR_DQ', context=_context)
630	            return STR_DQ
631	        else: # == 'STR_SQ'
632	            STR_SQ = self._scan('STR_SQ', context=_context)
633	            return STR_SQ
634	
635	
636	def parse(rule, text):
637	    P = XPathParser(XPathParserScanner(text))
638	    return wrap_error_reporter(P, rule)
639	
640	if __name__ == '__main__':
641	    from sys import argv, stdin
642	    if len(argv) >= 2:
643	        if len(argv) >= 3:
644	            f = open(argv[2],'r')
645	        else:
646	            f = stdin
647	        print(parse(argv[1], f.read()))
648	    else: print ('Args:  <rule> [<filename>]', file=sys.stderr)
649	# End -- grammar generated by Yapps
650	'''
651	)
